defects.symptom: Bare `dummyindex install` (and `dummyindex ingest` / `context init` / bare `context rebuild`) silently destroys a curated index: features/INDEX.json is overwritten with raw community-N stubs (curated features orphaned on disk), tree.json LLM-enriched abstracts are wiped, meta.json created_at/indexed_commit/config are reset, and PROJECT.md / architecture/overview.md / conventions/naming.md are regenerated over curated edits. This is the mechanism behind the '0.25.0 install re-shattered the curated 14-feature taxonomy into 838 community-N features' incidents (evidence items 25, 40, 54, 56; triggers for 14/35/45). The /dummyindex-update skill makes it worse by instructing agents to run `dummyindex install` while claiming the per-repo step is 'NON-DESTRUCTIVE'.
defects.root_cause: REPRODUCED in a temp repo on HEAD (v0.25.0 source): after curating an index (features-rename community-0 -> auth-core, INFERRED; enriched tree abstract), `python3 -m dummyindex install --scope project --dir <repo>` ran `_auto_init_project` -> `build_all` unconditionally (install.py:178-179, 223). Result: INDEX.json features became ['community-0','community-1','community-2'] (auth-core gone, dir orphaned), tree enrichment string gone, meta created_at reset. `build_all` has no enriched-index guard anywhere: scaffold_features._write_all unconditionally rewrites features/INDEX.json (builder.py:280-302), runner.py:373 rewrites tree.json, runner.py:128 calls new_meta (resets created_at + indexed_commit). The only guard in the codebase, `_is_enriched_index`, lives inside the `--changed` path (incremental.py:137) — install, init/ingest, and bare `rebuild` (cli/rebuild.py:71-78, warning only printed for --full) all bypass it. install auto-init has existed since v0.13.4 (commit 636a3bf), so 'worked on 0.24.0' reports reflect that 0.24-era updates didn't run `install` against the repo, not a code difference: git diff v0.24.0..v0.25.0 touches only reconcile_gate/release tooling.
defects.proposed_fix: Make every full-build entry point respect the enriched guard. (a) Promote `_is_enriched_index` to a public `is_enriched_index(context_dir)` (export from dummyindex/context/build/__init__.py; keep the bias-to-preserve semantics and its tests). (b) installer/install.py `_auto_init_project`: when `<root>/.context` exists and `is_enriched_index` is True, call `rebuild_changed(root, dummyindex_version=PACKAGE_VERSION, ...)` (which takes the non-destructive refresh path) instead of `build_all`, and print 'curated index preserved — deterministic artefacts refreshed'; keep hooks + CLAUDE.md bootstrap steps unchanged. (c) cli/rebuild.py bare path: before `build_all`, check the guard; on an enriched index either route to the non-destructive refresh with a notice or exit 2 with 'curated index detected — pass --full to discard taxonomy + enrichment' (DESIGN DECISION: auto-route vs refuse; refusing is safer for scripts/hooks, auto-route matches --changed semantics — recommend refuse with explicit --full escape hatch). (d) cli/init.py (== `ingest`): refuse to overwrite an enriched index unless a new `--force` flag is passed (init means 'first build'; an enriched index means it isn't the first). (e) Update dummyindex/skills/update/SKILL.md lines 23/73-76 to describe the new preserved behavior, removing the false 'build_all(bootstrap=True) ... is non-destructive' claim. CLI-boundary I/O: keep all printing in installer/cli modules, guard logic in context/build.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/test_install.py: test_install_auto_init_preserves_enriched_index (prime repo via build_all, rename a feature in INDEX.json to a non-community id, run install(scope='project', project_dir=...), assert INDEX.json feature ids unchanged, tree.json untouched, meta created_at preserved); tests/cli/: test_bare_rebuild_refuses_on_enriched_index (exit code + message; --full proceeds with warning); test_init_refuses_on_enriched_index_without_force. Follow existing idioms in tests/test_install.py:324-415 and tests/context/build/test_incremental.py:147-252.

defects.symptom: Once features/INDEX.json has been shattered (by the install/init path above, or restored from a commit that contains the shattered version), every subsequent `rebuild --changed` runs the FULL destructive re-cluster even though all curated feature directories are still on disk — the non-destructive guard permanently self-disarms. This is why users observed '`rebuild --changed` re-shatters AGAIN' after recovering via `git checkout HEAD -- .context` (evidence items 14, 35, 45, 52: '838 community dirs created / curated features intact 13/13').
defects.root_cause: REPRODUCED on HEAD: after `install` overwrote INDEX.json with community-* entries, touching one source file and running `rebuild --changed` produced 'context rebuild: 0 added, 1 modified, 0 removed → rebuilt' — the full build path — while the curated `auth-core/` directory sat orphaned on disk. `_is_enriched_index` (incremental.py:167-216) reads ONLY `features/INDEX.json`; curated per-feature directories (`features/<non-community-id>/feature.json`, spec.md with `confidence: INFERRED`) are never consulted, so a clobbered INDEX.json makes the index look deterministic-only and the guard returns False.
defects.proposed_fix: Extend `_is_enriched_index` (or add `_has_curated_feature_dirs(context_dir)` called from it) to also scan `features/*/feature.json` on disk: any directory whose name/feature_id does not start with 'community-' or whose feature.json carries INFERRED confidence makes the index enriched, regardless of INDEX.json contents. Keep the existing bias-to-preserve error handling (never raise; unreadable -> True). When the dir-scan says enriched but INDEX.json says deterministic-only, the non-destructive path should also surface a one-line warning in the rebuild summary ('features/INDEX.json does not list curated feature dirs on disk — index desync; run refresh-indexes / restore INDEX.json') so the user learns the INDEX is broken instead of silently refreshing forever. Cheap: one glob over features/*/feature.json, reading only feature_id + confidence.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_incremental.py: test_is_enriched_index_true_when_curated_dir_on_disk_but_index_shattered (write INDEX.json with only community-* entries + a features/auth-core/feature.json; assert guard True); test_rebuild_changed_preserves_when_index_shattered_but_dirs_curated (full integration: prime, shatter INDEX.json, touch source, rebuild_changed -> preserved_enriched True, no community dirs recreated).

defects.symptom: `rebuild --changed` reports 'no source files changed; .context/ unchanged.' while map/files.json is missing entire modules — the documented catch-up command is a silent no-op exactly when the index needs catching up. Hit after recovering a curated index with `git checkout -- .context` (tracked backbone restored to OLD state, gitignored cache/manifest.json left NEW). Users hand-deleted manifest entries to force re-extraction (evidence items 18, 21, 26).
defects.root_cause: REPRODUCED on HEAD: created app/billing/refunds.py, ran install (build_all stamped cache/manifest.json AND map/files.json with it), then `git checkout -- .context && git clean -fdq .context` to recover the curated index — manifest still listed refunds.py (cache/ is gitignored via runner.py:297 '_MANAGED_GITIGNORE_PATTERNS'), map/files.json did not. `rebuild --changed` printed 'no source files changed; .context/ unchanged.' and map/files.json still had 0 refunds.py entries. Root cause: the quick-exit (incremental.py:131-132) gates solely on a hash-diff against the gitignored manifest (`_read_prior_fingerprints_via_manifest`, incremental.py:114/252-266) and never validates that the tracked backbone (map/files.json) actually covers the manifest's files. Any flow where the manifest is newer than the backbone (git restore of .context, interrupted build) becomes a permanent silent gap. Nuance: a genuinely-new file NOT in the manifest IS detected (fresh detect() walk at incremental.py:95) and the enriched refresh DOES fold it into the map — confirmed; the no-op only persists until some unrelated file changes, but in real sessions that meant the gap survived multiple sessions.
defects.proposed_fix: In `rebuild_changed`, before the skip at incremental.py:131, add a coverage check: read map/files.json paths via the existing `_read_prior_fingerprints(files_json)` helper and compute `manifest_paths - files_json_paths` restricted to code files; if non-empty, do NOT skip — fall through to the (guarded) refresh, and include those paths in the ChangeSet as added/modified so the summary explains why it ran ('backbone missing N manifest-tracked files'). Keep it cheap: set difference on already-parsed dicts, no hashing. This also self-heals the desync because the enriched refresh re-derives map/files.json from a fresh walk (enriched_refresh.py:95-110). Note for implementer: docs-only paths appear in the manifest but never in files.json (which is code-only) — restrict the coverage check to paths present in the current detect() code set to avoid false positives.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_incremental.py: test_manifest_backbone_desync_forces_refresh (prime repo, curate INDEX.json, append a new code file's entry to cache/manifest.json while leaving map/files.json stale — or build then overwrite files.json with the pre-file version — run rebuild_changed; assert skipped is False and map/files.json contains the file afterwards); test_doc_only_manifest_entries_do_not_force_refresh (regression guard for the docs-in-manifest nuance).

defects.symptom: On a curated index, `context check` reports the identical drift forever ('drift detected — N added, M modified') even after a complete, verified reconcile, and every `rebuild --changed` re-runs the entire deterministic refresh for the same already-processed changes. SessionStart hooks and humans see permanently 'dirty' state, feeding the 'three mutually contradictory staleness oracles' confusion (evidence items 22, contributes to 19/28).
defects.root_cause: REPRODUCED on HEAD: after an enriched-path `rebuild --changed` (output 'enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s)'), an immediate second run with zero edits re-ran the full refresh again, and `context check` still reported '0 added, 1 modified' for the file processed two runs earlier. `write_manifest` is called only from `build_all` (runner.py:251) — `refresh_deterministic_artifacts` re-derives map/files.json, symbols, naming, source-docs, and symbol-graph from a fresh walk but never re-stamps cache/manifest.json, so the change-detection baseline (used by both incremental.py:114 and cli/check.py:68 via manifest.compare) never advances on the non-destructive path. On a curated index the manifest can only be advanced by the destructive build_all.
defects.proposed_fix: At the end of `refresh_deterministic_artifacts`, stamp the manifest with the same file set it just processed: reuse the in-scope `detection` to assemble code files + in-repo docs (mirror runner.py:246-251's manifest_files assembly, including the external-doc exclusion) and call `write_manifest(context_dir, root=root, files=manifest_files)` inside the same try/warn tolerance build_all uses. Then both `check` and the next `rebuild --changed` correctly report clean until a real edit. Note: this must land together with (or after) the Defect-3 coverage check, since stamping the manifest makes the quick-exit eager — the coverage check is the safety net for backbone/manifest desync from git operations.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/ (new test_enriched_refresh.py or extend test_incremental.py): test_enriched_refresh_restamps_manifest (prime + curate, edit a file, rebuild_changed -> preserved_enriched True; assert manifest.compare is clean afterwards and a second rebuild_changed returns skipped=True); tests/cli/test_check*: check exits 0/clean after an enriched-path refresh.

defects.symptom: meta.json `dummyindex_version` never advances on the non-destructive path — indexes stay stamped '0.15.0' forever after CLI upgrades (users hand-edited meta.json; the only sanctioned re-stamp is the destructive full rebuild). Additionally the destructive path resets `created_at`, erasing the index's ancestry (evidence items 12, 23, 41, 57).
defects.root_cause: REPRODUCED on HEAD: set meta dummyindex_version='0.15.0', ran enriched-path `rebuild --changed` — updated_at advanced but dummyindex_version stayed '0.15.0'. `rebuild_changed` accepts `dummyindex_version` (incremental.py:65) but the enriched branch (incremental.py:142-147) never passes it to `refresh_deterministic_artifacts`, which has no version parameter at all (enriched_refresh.py:73-78); `_refresh_meta` (enriched_refresh.py:208-211) calls `with_updates(file_count=..., symbol_count=...)` only. Separately, `build_all` always calls `new_meta` (runner.py:128) which resets created_at — reproduced: install on the curated repo moved created_at from 01:21:14 to 01:23:01.
defects.proposed_fix: (a) Add `dummyindex_version: str | None = None` to `refresh_deterministic_artifacts` and `_refresh_meta`; when provided and different, include it in `with_updates(...)` (meta.with_updates already re-stamps updated_at, meta.py:29-34). Thread it through from incremental.py:143 (the value is already in scope from cli/rebuild.py:50-55). (b) In `build_all`, before runner.py:128, attempt `read_meta(context_dir/'meta.json')` and, when present/readable, preserve its `created_at` via `.with_updates(created_at=existing.created_at, ...)` — wait, with_updates always bumps updated_at which is correct; pass created_at through the new_meta result with dataclasses.replace or an explicit with_updates(created_at=...). Keep frozen-dataclass immutability (no mutation; new Meta instances).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_meta.py / test_incremental.py: test_enriched_refresh_advances_dummyindex_version (meta stamped old version; rebuild_changed enriched path; assert meta.dummyindex_version == passed version and indexed_commit untouched); tests/context/build/test_runner.py: test_build_all_preserves_created_at_on_existing_index.

defects.symptom: `rebuild --changed` prints 'enriched index preserved' immediately followed by 'removed files: .context/features/<id>/flows/flow-057.json ...' — listing the index's own curated docs as removed, which reads as the rebuild having deleted council-authored flow docs and forced a manual git audit to disprove (evidence item 42).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED in code: `compute_reconcile_report` filters `.context/` paths out of `unassigned_new_files` only (`_is_context_path` applied at reconcile.py:110); `removed_files=tuple(sorted(delta.removed))` (reconcile.py:115) is unfiltered, so any commit since the anchor that deleted .context files (e.g. pruning flow docs during curation) surfaces them under 'removed files:' in the enriched-preserved summary printed by `_print_enriched_summary` (cli/rebuild.py:98-99). The index's own churn is reported as if it were source drift, directly contradicting the 'preserved' banner.
defects.proposed_fix: In `compute_reconcile_report`, filter `_is_context_path` from `delta.removed` before building `removed_files` (one-line generator filter, mirroring the unassigned filter at reconcile.py:110). Optionally also filter `delta.modified`/`delta.removed` before the drifted-feature mapping loop (currently harmless since `_file_owners` only contains source paths, but cheap belt-and-suspenders). Keep the dataclass frozen/immutable construction.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_reconcile.py: test_removed_context_files_not_reported (anchor at commit A, commit B deletes .context/features/x/flows/flow-001.md and a real source file; assert removed_files contains only the source path).

defects.symptom: Two parallel confidence systems confuse users and agents: fully-councilled features still show `confidence: EXTRACTED` in feature.json and the features/INDEX.md confidence column, while the real enrichment signal hides in spec.md markdown headers — prompting 'was there a recouncil?' doubt and hand-audits (evidence items 16, 46).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED in code: `rename_feature`/`merge_feature` set ConfidenceLevel.INFERRED on feature.json + INDEX.json (ops.py:81, 111, 258, 286), but the council's enrichment completion verb `mark_enriched` (placement.py:~360-379) only unlinks the `.pending-enrichment` marker — it never updates feature.json or the INDEX.json entry's confidence. A feature enriched in place (without rename/merge, the common recouncil path) therefore stays EXTRACTED everywhere machine-readable, and INDEX.md renders that stale value (indexes.py:161). Note: the claim that the NON-destructive rebuild resets INFERRED back to EXTRACTED is false — reproduced that INFERRED survives `rebuild --changed`; only the destructive full build replaces entries with stubs (Defect 1).
defects.proposed_fix: Make `mark_enriched` also set `confidence: ConfidenceLevel.INFERRED` in `features/<id>/feature.json` and the matching `features/INDEX.json` entry, then refresh INDEX.md via the existing `_refresh_index_artifacts`/`refresh_features_index_md` helpers (same pattern ops.py:95-116 uses for rename). Returns/result dataclasses stay frozen; reuse the JSON read-modify-write helpers already in the module. DESIGN DECISION: whether scaffold_feature/assign_files placements should stay EXTRACTED until mark-enriched (recommended: yes — the marker lifecycle then maps 1:1 to the confidence transition).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/ (features tests): test_mark_enriched_bumps_confidence (scaffold a feature dir with marker + EXTRACTED feature.json + INDEX entry; call mark_enriched; assert marker gone, feature.json and INDEX.json entry INFERRED, INDEX.md regenerated).

defects.symptom: After manual feature.json edits (e.g. rebase-conflict resolution), per-feature roll-up counts in features/INDEX.json and INDEX.md stay stale and no sanctioned verb resyncs them — `refresh-indexes` regenerates INDEX.md from INDEX.json (propagating the stale counts) and the graph from feature.json, so an agent hand-edited generated files to fix file_count 185→199 (evidence item 33).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED in code: placement.py:11 states the module hand-maintains features/INDEX.json ('no helper rebuilds it from disk'). The CLI ops (scaffold/assign/rename/merge) each patch INDEX.json incrementally, but nothing re-derives INDEX.json entries (member_count/file_count/flow_count/confidence) from the feature.json files on disk. `refresh_features_index_md` (indexes.py:19, used by cli/refresh.py) renders INDEX.md FROM INDEX.json (indexes.py:134-161), so when feature.json and INDEX.json disagree (any out-of-band edit: rebase resolution, manual curation), both index summaries stay wrong with no remediation other than hand-editing.
defects.proposed_fix: Extend `refresh-indexes` (cli/refresh.py + a new `resync_features_index(features_dir)` in indexes.py) to first re-derive each INDEX.json entry's counts/confidence/name from the corresponding `features/<id>/feature.json` (keeping entries whose dirs are missing, or flagging them), write INDEX.json, then regenerate INDEX.md and graph as today. Documenting it as the post-manual-edit remediation in the council reconcile docs (skills/council/65-reconcile.md) closes the workflow gap.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/features/test_indexes.py: test_refresh_indexes_resyncs_counts_from_feature_json (write feature.json with 5 files, INDEX.json entry claiming 3; run resync; assert INDEX.json + INDEX.md show 5).

defects.symptom: Generated stub docs leak the Python enum repr: spec.md/flow.md stubs read '_Deterministic stub (`confidence: ConfidenceLevel.EXTRACTED`)..._' instead of 'confidence: EXTRACTED' (visible in evidence item 35's committed stubs; reproduced in the temp repo).
defects.root_cause: REPRODUCED: `ConfidenceLevel(str, Enum)` formats as 'ConfidenceLevel.EXTRACTED' under f-strings on Python 3.11+ (str-mixin enums use Enum.__str__ since 3.11). render.py interpolates `{feat.confidence}` / `{flow.confidence}` directly; JSON output is unaffected (json.dumps serializes the str value), so only the markdown stubs leak.
defects.proposed_fix: Add `__str__ = str.__str__` to `ConfidenceLevel` in dummyindex/pipeline/enums.py (fixes every f-string site at once and matches pre-3.11 behavior), or switch the two render.py interpolations to `.value`. Prefer the enum-level fix; grep for other f-string interpolations of ConfidenceLevel to confirm coverage.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/features/test_render.py (or existing render test): assert _stub_feature_spec output contains 'confidence: EXTRACTED' and not 'ConfidenceLevel.'; tests/pipeline/test_enums.py: f'{ConfidenceLevel.EXTRACTED}' == 'EXTRACTED'.

defects.symptom: `rebuild --changed` for a handful of edited files prints a full-repo 'AST extraction: 1086/1086 files (100%)' progress crawl, making the 'incremental' path look like (and partially be) a full re-extraction (evidence item 37).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED in code: the enriched refresh re-runs `extract()` over ALL detected code files (enriched_refresh.py:97) — correctness comes from the per-file extraction cache, but the progress printer (pipeline/extract/__init__.py:156, 172) counts every file identically whether served from cache or re-parsed, so the output reports a full extraction pass. Work scales with repo size for hashing/cache lookups even when only a few files changed; the message actively misleads about what --changed does.
defects.proposed_fix: Track cache hits vs fresh parses inside `extract()` (the cache lookup already distinguishes them) and change the progress/summary lines to e.g. 'AST extraction: 1086 files (1075 cached, 11 parsed)'. Optionally suppress intermediate progress lines when fresh-parse count is small. Pure output change; no behavioral risk. (A true changed-files-only extraction feed is a larger design change — out of scope for this fix.)
defects.tests_to_add: tests/pipeline/extract tests: capsys assertion that a second extract() run over unchanged files reports the cached count (e.g. 'N cached, 0 parsed').

not_confirmed: Claimed direct regression in `rebuild --changed` between 0.24.0 and 0.25.0 (items 14/35/45/52 framing): NOT confirmed as a --changed code change. `git diff v0.24.0..v0.25.0` touches only reconcile_gate, release workflow, and git helpers — no rebuild/incremental/installer code. Reproduction shows the --changed guard works correctly on a curated INDEX.json at HEAD ('enriched index preserved'). The observed re-shattering is fully explained by the cascade: the 0.25.0 *update flow* ran `dummyindex install` (destructive auto-init, Defect 1), which shattered INDEX.json, after which the guard self-disarms (Defect 2). Same behavior would occur on 0.24.0 if install were run there.

Item 27 'full rebuild's file discovery is manifest-seeded, silently skips files newer than the prior manifest': mechanism NOT confirmed — `build_all` does a fresh `detect()` filesystem walk (runner.py:111) and map/files.json derives from that walk, not the manifest; the manifest is write-only output (runner.py:251). The 3-file extraction gap that session observed is real but its cause must be a detect()-level exclusion or path-resolution issue specific to that repo (could not reproduce in a clean repo; needs that repo's file set to investigate — likely belongs to a detection/extraction cluster).

Key-claim 5 'rebuild resets feature confidence from councilled/enriched back to EXTRACTED' as applied to the non-destructive path: NOT confirmed — reproduced that INFERRED confidence and renamed feature ids survive `rebuild --changed` (enriched path never touches feature.json/INDEX.json). What IS true: destructive full builds replace INDEX entries with EXTRACTED community stubs (covered by Defect 1), and councilled-in-place features were never INFERRED in feature.json to begin with because mark-enriched doesn't bump confidence (covered by the P2 confidence defect).

Item 3 (curated frontmatter owner '@karim' silently changed to '@ahmed'): no dummyindex code writes `owner:` frontmatter anywhere (grep over dummyindex/ has zero owner-frontmatter writers), so a rebuild/ingest stamping git-user as owner is ruled out; likely a manual/agent edit in a prior session. Not a dummyindex defect.

Item 57's specific 'updated_at predates the latest regeneration by ~2h': could not fully reproduce — the enriched refresh and build_all both re-stamp updated_at (verified). The created_at reset half of item 57 IS confirmed and covered by Defect 5. The updated_at gap most plausibly came from a `refresh-indexes`-style verb or recovery flow that rewrites artifacts without touching meta; no single code path confirmed.

Item 58 'rebuild --changed reports no feature drift after a sweeping migration': the reconcile report is a git-diff against meta.indexed_commit (reconcile.py:95); if the migration's commits were already behind a freshly-stamped anchor, an empty report is correct-by-design Model B behavior — no code defect confirmable from the evidence alone. The checklist-ticking half belongs to the build-loop cluster.
quick_wins: Add `__str__ = str.__str__` to ConfidenceLevel (dummyindex/pipeline/enums.py:16) — kills the 'ConfidenceLevel.EXTRACTED' leak in every generated stub (P3 defect above, ~1 line + tests).

Filter `_is_context_path` from `delta.removed` in compute_reconcile_report (dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:115) — one-line generator filter, stops .context churn being reported as 'removed files' under the 'enriched index preserved' banner.

Until the full Defect-1 guard lands: print the existing --full destructive warning (cli/rebuild.py:38-45) for the BARE `rebuild` path too (it currently fires only when --full is passed, yet bare rebuild is equally destructive) — ~3 lines.
Thread `dummyindex_version` from incremental.py:143 into refresh_deterministic_artifacts/_refresh_meta (Defect 5a) — ~6 lines across two functions, immediately unfreezes the 0.15.0 version stamps.

skills/update/SKILL.md:76 — delete/correct the false claim that install's `build_all(bootstrap=True)` is 'non-destructive' (doc-only, prevents the update skill from prescribing the data-loss path until the code fix ships).
